data.narrative:
iQPS is a publicly traded Space & Aerospace company founded by Tetsuo Yasaka, Noboru Sakurai, and Kunihiro Funakoshi. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage peers: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M).

Peer-median valuation is the applicable triangulation method here. The median across the four public Space & Aerospace comparables yields $6.7B, with a range spanning $900M to $37.0B. A public-comp P/S approach using the sector median of 64.2x cannot be applied due to the absence of target revenue data on file. This limitation is standard for private-company valuation but does not constrain the peer-multiple method, which relies on discrete transaction valuations rather than financial ratios.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.7B (low) to $7.6B (high), centered on $6.7B median. Key uncertainties include the wide spread within the peer set—a 40x variance from floor to ceiling—reflecting material heterogeneity in launch capability, orbital service offerings, and market maturity across comparables. The concentration of valuation weight among two outliers (Rocket Lab and Astroscale) versus the tighter mid-market clustering of Planet Labs and Firefly Aerospace suggests sensitivity to business model definition and operational stage. Revenue transparency would refine this estimate materially.
